methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $5M+, founded by Lucas Young and Thomas Li. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $5M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $12M. Peer-Median analysis across four same-sector comparables—Tenna Systems ($60M, Seed), Ares Industries ($150M, Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M, Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M, Series A)—produces a median of $225M with a range of $60M to $500M.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $11M (low) to $259M (high), with a median implied value of $119M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty in stage-appropriate valuation for an early-stage company in a capital-intensive sector. Key sensitivities include Deepnight's specific technology differentiation relative to the peer set, path-to-Series A timing and terms, and customer traction or military validation achieved since the $5M raise. The Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively; the Peer-Median approach incorporates later-stage comparables and thus skews higher.
